creator: brett@peloton.physics.montana.edu
text:
This is a new port of tkfont - a Tk based replacement for
	xfontsel.  It works really nicely and doesn't have the 
	limitations that xfontsel has (like if you have a LOT of
	fonts installed for The Gimp, xfontsel can't scroll to
	see them all).  Port has been portlinted and run through
	the standard tests (make, make package, make deinstall, 
	pkg_add and pkg_del).

Fix: #!/bin/csh -f
# Uuencoded gz-compressed .tar file created by csh script  uufiles
# For more info (11/95), see e.g. http://xxx.lanl.gov/faq/uufaq.html
# If you are on a unix machine this file will unpack itself: strip
# any mail header and call resulting file, e.g., tkfont.uu
# (uudecode ignores these header lines and starts at begin line below)
# Then say        csh tkfont.uu
# or explicitly execute the commands (generally more secure):
#    uudecode tkfont.uu ;   gunzip tkfont.tar.gz ;
#    tar -xvf tkfont.tar
# On some non-unix (e.g. VAX/VMS), first use editor to change filename
# in "begin" line below to tkfont.tar-gz , then execute
#    uudecode tkfont.uu
#    gzip -d tkfont.tar-gz
#    tar -xvf tkfont.tar
#
uudecode $0
chmod 644 tkfont.tar.gz
gunzip -c tkfont.tar.gz | tar -xvf -
rm $0 tkfont.tar.gz
exit
How-To-Repeat: 
	port included below in a uuencoded tarball
